summary: Understand where this preparation fits and what support is available to you as a chaplaincy volunteer.
objectives: Know what this course does and does not replace.
Understand that local procedures will be covered with your mentor.
Know that volunteers are supported and are not expected to manage difficult situations alone.

sections.title: Welcome to the team
body: Chaplaincy volunteers offer time, attention and a compassionate human presence to patients and, where appropriate, to their families. You will meet people from many religious, spiritual and non-religious backgrounds.
This course gives all UHL and LPT chaplaincy volunteers the same foundation. It introduces the role, the skills you will use and the boundaries that keep patients and volunteers safe.

items.title: This course
items.body: Chaplaincy-specific preparation: presence, listening, consent, boundaries, appropriate referral and responding to concerns.

items.title: Trust training
items.body: The mandatory learning required by your Trust, including subjects such as information governance, safeguarding and infection prevention.

items.title: Local induction
items.body: Your mentor will show you where to sign in, who to contact, how local systems work and what is expected in your particular placement.

items.title: Ongoing support
items.body: Questions, uncertainty and emotional reactions are normal. Bring them to your mentor or another chaplain rather than carrying them alone.

sections.label: Remember
sections.title: You do not need to have all the answers
body: Good volunteering is not about solving every problem. It is about being attentive, knowing the limits of your role and involving the right person when more support is needed.

sections.quote: The patient sets the agenda. Our task is to make room for what matters to them.
sections.caption: A guiding principle for the whole course
